summary: The test `parallel/test-debugger-exec` failed because the Node.js debugger did not emit the expected 'break on start' message within the 15-second timeout. The debugger attached successfully but never paused at the initial breakpoint, causing `waitForInitialBreak` to time out.
root_cause: The test `test/parallel/test-debugger-exec.js` timed out (15000 ms) while waiting for the pattern `/break (?:on start )?in/i` from the debugger. The debugger connected and attached but never sent the initial break notification, so `waitForInitialBreak` threw a Timeout error.

evidence.quote: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i
evidence.reason: This is the explicit timeout error thrown by the test helper, showing the debugger never emitted the expected initial break message within 15 seconds.
The stack trace shows the timeout originated in `waitForInitialBreak`, confirming the debugger did not break on start as expected.

evidence.quote:
output: '< Debugger listening on ws://127.0.0.1:49508/1fd32687-38b3-450e-96e5-4a28a88f601e\n' +
    '< For help, see: https://nodejs.org/en/docs/inspector\n' +
    '< \n' +
    'connecting to 127.0.0.1:49508 ...\n' +
    ' ok\n' +
    '< Debugger attached.\n' +
    '< \n' +
    '\n' +
    'debug> '
evidence.reason: The debugger output shows it connected and attached successfully, but the 'break in ...' line that `waitForInitialBreak` expects was never received, confirming the initial breakpoint was not hit.
Confirms exactly one test failed in the test run.
The make test-ci target exited with an error due to the test failure, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Investigate why the Node.js debugger is not emitting the initial break notification on macOS arm64 (the runner is on arm64 with Xcode 16.1). This may be a timing or platform-specific regression in the inspector/debugger code. Consider increasing the TIMEOUT in `test/common/debugger.js` as a short-term workaround, or investigate the debugger's `--inspect-brk` behavior on this platform.
